spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (document.querySelector) with runtime script (~10605 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 4 buttons, 1 inputs, 1 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (algorithm, behaviour, build, child, chrome, drag, dropeffect, dropzones, effectallowed, multiple, negotiation).

edge_cases.rationale: Some failure / fallback handling shown (3 hits), but the demo skews toward the happy path and doesn't surface most edge cases the spec calls out.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: 1 button rule(s) override `background` without also overriding `color`. The global button rule sets both, so this leaves the text colour inherited from the dark default (CLAUDE.md rule #6).

openQuestions.title: Demo at least one explicit failure or edge case
openQuestions.detail: Spec / chromestatus motivation mentions failure modes worth showing — permission denial, missing-feature, empty input, malformed payload. Add a control or scripted scenario that walks the user through one and shows the resulting error or fallback.

openQuestions.title: Pair every button background override with a colour override
openQuestions.detail: Audit the inline `<style>` block: each `button { background: ... }` (or `.foo-btn { background: ... }`) needs an explicit `color: ...` next to it. CLAUDE.md rule #6 documents the light-on-light invisibility bug this causes.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The drag and drop specifications require the "dataTransfer"'s object "dropEffect" attribute to have certain predetermined values on "dragEnter", "dragOver" and "dragLeave". Drag enter and drag over should have a "dropEffect" based on the cu") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/dnd.html#dndevents to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~10605 chars of script across 4 button(s) and 2 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 2 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
